process_name: Direct CP violation in K -> pi pi
implementation_difficulty_reason: Requires new Delta S = 1 WET/SMEFT matching, RG evolution, K -> pi pi matrix elements, and treatment of electroweak/QCD penguin plus chromomagnetic operators; the repo's Delta F = 2 machinery is not a direct evaluator.

commands: rg -n "epsilon_prime|eps_prime|epsilon.?/epsilon|varepsilon.?/varepsilon|K.?->.?pi.?pi|KtoPiPi|Delta S.?=.?1|DeltaS1|chromomagnetic|O_8g|Q_6|Q_8" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "epsilon.?prime|eps.?prime|epsilon'|varepsilon.?prime|epsilon\s*/\s*epsilon|eps\s*/\s*eps|K.*pi.*pi|pi.*pi.*K|chromomagnetic|penguin|Q_6|Q_8|Delta I|DeltaI|A0|A2|Re A0|Re A2" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'
evidence: No direct K003 matches from the focused grep command; exit status 1.
quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:1 implements a Delta F = 2 benchmark observable layer, not Delta S = 1 K -> pi pi amplitudes.
quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:209 lists epsilon_K, B_d, B_s, and D mixing inputs.
quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23 enumerates only epsilon_K, K, B_d, B_s, and D0 policy systems.

sections.process:
\textbf{Standard notation:} \(\mathrm{Re}(\varepsilon'/\varepsilon)\).
This entry covers direct CP violation in the two-pion decay amplitudes of
neutral kaons, commonly written through
\(\mathrm{Re}(\varepsilon'/\varepsilon)=(1-|\eta_{00}/\eta_{+-}|)/3\).
The process id is \(K003\).
sections.pdg_prose:
The canonical PDG-sidecar value is
\[
  \mathrm{Re}(\varepsilon'/\varepsilon)=0.00166\pm0.00023
  = (16.6\pm2.3)\times 10^{-4}.
\]
This number is recorded in the YAML \texttt{pdg\_or\_equivalent} block and
traces to
\texttt{flavor\_catalog/references/K003/pdg\_live\_S013\_20260516.txt}.
The companion PDG DataBlock snapshot records \(\texttt{OUR FIT}=1.67\pm0.23\)
and \(\texttt{OUR AVERAGE}=1.68\pm0.20\), both in units of \(10^{-3}\).  The
same sidecar lists the two dominant experimental inputs: KTeV
\((19.2\pm2.1)\times10^{-4}\) and NA48
\((14.7\pm2.2)\times10^{-4}\).
sections.relevance:
Unlike \(\varepsilon_K\), this is a \(\Delta S=1\) nonleptonic observable.
It tests CP-odd short-distance contributions to the \(K\to\pi\pi\) amplitudes
after they are filtered through long-distance QCD.  In warped or composite
flavor models with anarchic Yukawas, the dangerous effects are not the
repo's current neutral-meson-mixing operators but new penguin, scalar/tensor,
and dipole structures.  BSM master-formula work identifies strong sensitivity
to electroweak left-right penguin \(Q_8\), QCD penguin \(Q_6\), and
chromo-magnetic \(O_{8g}\)-type operators, making the channel a natural
stress test for UV completions with non-minimal chirality structure.
sections.post_2008:
The experimental average has not been replaced by a newer kaon experiment, but
KTeV's final \(K\to\pi\pi\) result was published after the CFW-era baseline.
The main post-2008 changes are theoretical.  RBC/UKQCD produced a
physical-kinematics lattice calculation of \(A_0\) and \(\varepsilon'\), with
\(\mathrm{Re}(\varepsilon'/\varepsilon)=
21.7(2.6)(6.2)(5.0)\times10^{-4}\).  Aebischer, Bobeth, and Buras then
reanalysed the ratio with NNLO electroweak-penguin corrections and ChPT
isospin-breaking inputs, obtaining
\((17.4\pm6.1)\times10^{-4}\) in the octet treatment and
\((13.9\pm5.2)\times10^{-4}\) in the nonet treatment.  In parallel, 2019
BSM master-formula papers organized the \(\Delta S=1\) operator dependence
in WET/SMEFT language rather than treating \(\varepsilon'/\varepsilon\) as a
single phenomenological number.
sections.validity:
As a catalog constraint, \(K003\) is highly informative but not plug-and-play.
The experimental number is cleanly established, while the SM subtraction and
BSM interpretation depend on hadronic matrix elements, isospin breaking,
operator matching, and RG evolution.  It should be classified as a
theory-limited, model-dependent \(\Delta S=1\) amplitude constraint rather
than a robust tree-level exclusion like the current \(\Delta F=2\) kaon lane.
sections.code_coverage_prose:
\textbf{NO.}  Greps over \texttt{quarkConstraints/}, \texttt{qcd/},
\texttt{flavorConstraints/}, \texttt{neutrinos/}, \texttt{yukawa/},
\texttt{warpConfig/}, \texttt{solvers/}, \texttt{scanParams/}, and
\texttt{tests/} found no direct implementation of
\(\varepsilon'/\varepsilon\), \(K\to\pi\pi\), \(\Delta S=1\), \(Q_6\),
\(Q_8\), or \(O_{8g}\).  The nearby kaon code is explicitly a
\(\Delta F=2\) benchmark slice in \texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:1},
with default inputs for \(\varepsilon_K\) at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:209} and an
\(\varepsilon_K\) evaluator at \texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:729}.
The modern policy surface likewise enumerates only
\(\varepsilon_K\), \(K\), \(B_d\), \(B_s\), and \(D^0\) systems at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23}.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
\textbf{HIGH.}  A production constraint would require a new \(\Delta S=1\)
operator basis, matching from the warped/composite UV model to WET/SMEFT
coefficients, RG running to hadronic scales, and lattice/ChPT inputs for
\(K\to\pi\pi\) matrix elements.  The current \(\Delta F=2\) VLL/VRR/LR
machinery cannot be reused as a direct evaluator.
